acceptance_criteria: Any citation in the synthesized answer must reference a file path that was actually present in the selected-file input set; citations to unknown paths are flagged/rejected.

rows.criterion: Citation matching a provided file path is not flagged
Hallucinated citation (no matching '## File:' header in selected_markdown) is detected/flagged
Mixed valid + hallucinated citation set: only the hallucinated one is flagged, valid one is not
Detection is order-independent (hallucinated citation first vs. second in answer/citations)
Flagging surfaces to the caller as a distinct signal without silently mutating/dropping the raw citations list
End-to-end via synthesize_answer() + LLMClient-mocked fixture (not unit-testing unknown_citations() on a hand-built SynthesizerResult in isolation)
rows.test: all tests in file call synthesize_answer() through _FakeLLMClient

note: General unknown_citations() correctness (dedup, order-preservation) is already covered by test_synthesizer.py (4.5.1) and intentionally not re-duplicated here; this file's tests are scoped to the specific one-valid+one-hallucinated end-to-end scenario 4.5.2's acceptance criteria/test spec describe.
